title: What A Friend We Have In Jesus

c_lines: Music: 'untitled' Charles C. Converse, 1868.  Setting: "Book of hymns and tunes", 1874.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2005 Revision.

1.raw_text: What a Friend we have in Jesus, all our sins and griefs to bear! What a privi- lege to carry everything to God in prayer! O what peace we often forfeit, O what needless pain we bear, All because we do not carry everything to God in prayer.

2.raw_text: Have we trials and temptations? Is there trouble anywhere? We should never be discouraged; take it to the Lord in prayer. Can we find a friend so faithful who will all our sorrows share? Jesus knows our every weakness; take it to the Lord in prayer.

3.raw_text: Are we weak and heavy laden, cumbered with a load of care? Precious Savior, still our refuge, take it to the Lord in prayer. Do your friends despise, forsake you? Take it to the Lord in prayer! In His arms He'll take and shield you; you will find a solace there.

4.raw_text: Blessed Savior, Thou hast promised Thou wilt all our burdens bear May we ever, Lord, be bringing all to Thee in earnest prayer. Soon in glory bright unclouded there will be no need for prayer Rapture, praise and endless worship will be our sweet portion there.
